label: Government bond yields
use: Anchor for discount rates, curve shape, and sector leadership.

label: Inflation and breakevens
use: Explains whether rates are moving because of price pressure or growth stress.

label: Central bank and policy expectations
use: Shapes the front-end of the curve and financing conditions.

label: Equity leadership and breadth
use: Shows whether rates pressure is spilling into growth, banks, defensives, or cyclicals.

label: Volume and liquidity
use: Separates high-conviction moves from weak participation.

label: Events and catalysts
use: Overrides slower factors when policy, war, deals, or earnings shocks dominate.

label: Relation graph
use: Maps transmission between companies, sectors, and related entities.
